info.title: Paperless-AI next API Documentation
info.description: API documentation for the Paperless-AI next application

BearerAuth.description:
JWT-based authentication for web app users. The token is obtained by authenticating via the login endpoint.

### How to authenticate:
1. Send a POST request to `/login` with your username and password
2. The server will respond with a JWT token (also set as a cookie in browsers)
3. Include this token in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer {token}`

Example:
```
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
```

JWT tokens are valid for 24 hours after issuance.

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description:
API key authentication for programmatic access. The API key can be generated or regenerated using the /api/key-regenerate endpoint.

### How to authenticate:
1. Access the API key from your application settings
2. Include the API key in the `x-api-key` HTTP header for all requests

Example:
```
x-api-key: 7c1f3f5e2b0a9d8c6e4b2a1d3f5e8c9b2a1d3f5e
```

API keys do not expire unless regenerated.

content.example: This is an invoice from Company XYZ...

custom_fields.description: Custom field values for the document

created_at.description: Date and time when the processing occurred

link.description: Link to the document in Paperless-ngx

custom_fields.description: Custom field values for the document

promptTokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt

completionTokens.description: Number of tokens in the completion

recordsTotal.description: Total number of records in database

get.description:
Returns the complete OpenAPI specification for the Paperless-AI next API.
This endpoint attempts to serve a static OpenAPI JSON file first, falling back
to dynamically generating the specification if the file cannot be read.

The OpenAPI specification document contains all API endpoints, parameters,
request bodies, responses, and schemas for the entire application.

302.description: Redirect to login when authentication is missing or invalid

500.description: Server error occurred while retrieving the OpenAPI specification

get.description: Backward-compatible redirect to `/api-docs/openapi.json`.

get.summary: Root endpoint that redirects to the dashboard
get.description:
This endpoint serves as the entry point for the application.
When accessed, it automatically redirects the user to the dashboard page.
No parameters or authentication are required for this redirection.

Provides information about the current system health status.
This endpoint checks database connectivity and returns system operational status.
Used for monitoring and automated health checks.

get.summary: Render login page or redirect to setup if no users exist
get.description:
Serves the login page for user authentication to the Paperless-AI next application.
If no users exist in the database, the endpoint automatically redirects to the setup page
to complete the initial application configuration.

This endpoint handles both new user sessions and returning users whose
sessions have expired.

schema.description: HTML content of the login page

302.description: Redirect to setup page if no users exist, or to dashboard if already authenticated

post.summary: Authenticate user with username and password
post.description:
Authenticates a user using their username and password credentials.
The endpoint supports a preparation flow for MFA:
first step validates credentials and starts an MFA challenge,
second step accepts a TOTP authentication code and completes sign-in.
If authentication is successful, a JWT token is generated and stored in a secure HTTP-only
cookie for subsequent requests.

Failed login attempts are logged for security purposes, and multiple failures
may result in temporary account lockout depending on configuration.

mfaStep.description: Set to '1' when submitting the MFA step

mfaToken.description: One-time code entered in the MFA verification step

200.description: Login page rendered again for invalid credentials, pending MFA verification, or invalid MFA code

302.description: Authentication successful and redirected to dashboard

429.description: Too many login attempts; login temporarily rate-limited

get.summary: Log out user and clear JWT cookie
get.description:
Terminates the current user session by invalidating and clearing the JWT authentication
cookie. After logging out, the user is redirected to the login page.

This endpoint also clears any session-related data stored on the server side
for the current user.

302.description: Logout successful, redirected to login page

schema.description: HTTP-only cookie with cleared JWT token and immediate expiration

get.summary: Get sample data for a document
get.description:
Retrieves sample data extracted from a document, including processed text content
and any metadata that has been extracted or processed by the AI.

This endpoint is commonly used for previewing document data in the UI before
completing document processing or updating metadata.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Document ID to retrieve sample data for

content.description: Extracted text content from the document
content.example: Invoice from Acme Corp. Total amount: $125.00, Due date: 2023-08-15

metadata.description: Any metadata that has been extracted from the document

get.description:
Renders the AI playground page for experimenting with document analysis.

This interactive environment allows users to test different AI providers and prompts
on document content without affecting the actual document processing workflow.
Users can paste document text, customize prompts, and see raw AI responses
to better understand how the AI models analyze document content.

The playground is useful for fine-tuning prompts and testing AI capabilities
before applying them to actual document processing.

schema.description: HTML content of the AI playground interface

get.summary: Search recent documents for omnibox selectors
get.description: Returns recent documents filtered by query for Manual/OCR document selectors.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text query matched against document id, title, and correspondent name.

get.description: Returns documents and metadata required to initialize the AI playground UI.

Retrieves the thumbnail image for a specific document from the Paperless-ngx system.
This endpoint proxies the request to the Paperless-ngx API and returns the thumbnail
image for display in the UI.

The thumbnail is returned as an image file in the format provided by Paperless-ngx,
typically JPEG or PNG.

parameters.name: documentId
parameters.description: ID of the document to retrieve thumbnail for

500.description: Server error or Paperless-ngx connection failure

get.description:
Renders the document history page with filtering options.
This page displays a list of all documents that have been processed by Paperless-AI,
showing the changes made to the documents through AI processing.

The page includes filtering capabilities by correspondent, tag, and free text search,
allowing users to easily find specific documents or categories of processed documents.
Each entry includes links to the original document in Paperless-ngx.

schema.description: HTML content of the history page with filtering controls and document list

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of documents that have been processed by Paperless-AI.
Supports filtering by tag, correspondent, and search term.
Designed for integration with DataTables jQuery plugin.

This endpoint provides comprehensive information about each processed document,
including its metadata before and after AI processing, allowing users to track
changes made by the system.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Number of records to return per page

parameters.name: search[value]
parameters.description: Global search term (searches title, correspondent and tags)

parameters.name: order[0][column]
parameters.description: Index of column to sort by (0=document_id, 1=title, etc.)

recordsTotal.description: Total number of records in the database

created_at.description: Date and time when the processing occurred

link.description: Link to the document in Paperless-ngx

get.summary: Load history data with progress updates (Server-Sent Events)
get.description:
Preloads history and tag data with real-time progress updates via SSE.
This endpoint should be called before displaying the history table to warm up the cache.
Requires authentication via JWT token or API key.

schema.example:
data: {"type":"progress","percentage":10,"message":"Loading history entries..."}

data: {"type":"complete","message":"Loaded 150 documents with 25 tags","count":150}

post.description:
Deletes all processing records from the database, allowing documents to be processed again.
This doesn't delete the actual documents from Paperless-ngx, only their processing status in Paperless-AI.

This operation can be useful when changing AI models or prompts, as it allows reprocessing
all documents with the updated configuration.
Forces cache invalidation to load fresh filter data on next request

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description: Returns full stored AI output details and a live diff view against current Paperless metadata.

post.description: Restores title, tags, correspondent and related metadata from saved original values.
Removes all tracking records for a document so it is processed again in a subsequent scan.

post.summary: Manually clear the centralized tag cache
post.description: Forces the tag cache to refresh on next access. Useful after external tag modifications.

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded (max 10 requests per 15 minutes)

get.description: Returns current thumbnail cache count and total size from local cache storage.

post.description: Deletes all cached thumbnail PNG files from local cache storage and returns cleanup stats.

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Removes local runtime override values so injected environment variables are used after restart.
This operation is restricted to interactive user sessions and requires the current account password.

currentPassword.description: Current password of the signed-in settings user

post.description:
Triggers an immediate reconciliation pass that removes stale entries from the
local AI database for documents that have been deleted in Paperless-ngx.
Uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream real-time progress.
Returns a single result event with the number of removed entries.

Deletes processing records for specific documents, allowing them to be processed again.
This doesn't delete the actual documents from Paperless-ngx, only their processing status in Paperless-AI.

This operation is useful when you want to reprocess only selected documents after changes to
the AI model, prompt, or document metadata configuration.

ids.description: Array of document IDs to reset

get.summary: Validate history entries against Paperless-ngx (Server-Sent Events)
get.description:
Checks each history entry stored locally and verifies the corresponding document still exists in Paperless-ngx.
Uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream real-time progress updates.
Processes documents in parallel batches (50 at a time) for faster validation.
Returns progress updates and final list of missing documents.

post.description:
Initiates an immediate scan of documents in Paperless-ngx that haven't been processed yet.
This endpoint can be used to manually trigger processing without waiting for the scheduled interval.

The scan will:
- Connect to Paperless-ngx API
- Fetch all unprocessed documents
- Process each document with the configured AI service
- Update documents in Paperless-ngx with generated metadata

The process respects the function limitations set in the configuration.

post.summary: Request graceful stop for active scan
post.description:
Requests a graceful stop of the currently running scan.
The current document is allowed to finish processing before the scan exits.

Generates a new random API key for the application and updates the .env file.
The previous API key will be invalidated immediately after generation.

This API key can be used for programmatic access to the API endpoints
by sending it in the `x-api-key` header of subsequent requests.

**Security Notice**: This operation invalidates any existing API key.
All systems using the previous key will need to be updated.

get.description:
Renders the application setup page for initial configuration.

This page allows configuring the connection to Paperless-ngx, AI services,
and other application settings. It loads existing configuration if available
and redirects to dashboard if setup is already complete.

The setup page is the entry point for new installations and guides users through
the process of connecting to Paperless-ngx, configuring AI providers, and setting up
admin credentials.

schema.description: HTML content of the application setup page

302.description: Redirects to dashboard if setup is already complete

get.summary: Get AI provider presets for initial setup

post.summary: Test Paperless-ngx connectivity and permissions during setup
Fetch Paperless-ngx counts and tags for setup wizard
Test AI provider credentials during setup
Discover available AI models during setup
Test OCR provider connectivity during setup
Discover available OCR models during setup
Finalize initial setup, persist env config, and trigger restart

get.description:
Fetches and returns the content of a specific document from Paperless-ngx 
for preview in the manual document review interface.

This endpoint retrieves document details including content, title, ID, and tags,
allowing users to view the document text before applying changes or processing
it with AI tools. The document content is retrieved directly from Paperless-ngx
using the system's configured API credentials.

tags.description: Array of tag names assigned to the document

500.description: Server error or Paperless connection error

get.description:
Renders the manual document review page that allows users to browse, 
view and manually process documents from Paperless-ngx.

This interface enables users to review documents, view their content, and 
manage tags, correspondents, and document metadata without AI assistance.
Users can apply manual changes to documents based on their own judgment,
which is particularly useful for correction or verification of AI-processed documents.

200.description: Manual document review page rendered successfully

schema.description: HTML content of the manual document review interface

get.description:
Retrieves all tags from Paperless-ngx for use in the manual document review interface.

This endpoint returns a complete list of all available tags that can be applied to documents,
including their IDs, names, and colors. The tags are retrieved directly from Paperless-ngx
and used for tag selection in the UI when manually updating document metadata.

500.description: Server error or Paperless connection error

get.description:
Retrieves all documents from Paperless-ngx for display in the manual document review interface.

This endpoint returns a list of all available documents that can be manually reviewed,
including their basic metadata such as ID, title, and creation date. The documents are
retrieved directly from Paperless-ngx and presented in the UI for selection and processing.

500.description: Server error or Paperless connection error

get.description:
Retrieves the list of correspondents with their document counts.
This endpoint returns all correspondents in the system along with 
the number of documents associated with each correspondent.

200.description: List of correspondents with document counts retrieved successfully

count.description: Number of documents associated with this correspondent

get.description:
Retrieves the list of tags with their document counts.
This endpoint returns all tags in the system along with 
the number of documents associated with each tag.

200.description: List of tags with document counts retrieved successfully

count.description: Number of documents associated with this tag

post.description:
Processes incoming webhook notifications from Paperless-ngx about document
changes, additions, or deletions. The webhook allows Paperless-AI next to respond
to document changes in real-time.

When a new document is added or updated in Paperless-ngx, this endpoint can
trigger automatic AI processing for metadata extraction.

processing_queued.description: Whether AI processing was queued for this document

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Renders the main dashboard page of the application with summary statistics and visualizations.
The dashboard provides an overview of processed documents, system metrics, and important statistics
about document processing including tag counts, correspondent counts, and token usage.

The page displays visualizations for document processing status, token distribution, 
processing time statistics, and document type categorization to help administrators
understand system performance and document processing patterns.

schema.description: HTML content of the dashboard page

get.description: Returns all aggregate counters and chart datasets required by the dashboard UI.

Renders the application settings page where users can modify configuration
after initial setup.

This page allows administrators to update connections to Paperless-ngx, 
AI provider settings, processing parameters, feature toggles, and custom fields.
The interface provides validation for connection settings and displays the current
configuration values.

Changes made on this page require application restart to take full effect.

schema.description: HTML content of the application settings page

post.description:
Updates the configuration settings of the Paperless-AI next application after initial setup.
This endpoint allows administrators to modify connections to Paperless-ngx, 
AI provider settings, processing parameters, and feature toggles.

Changes made through this endpoint are applied immediately and affect all future
document processing operations.

paperlessUsername.description: Username for Paperless-ngx (alternative to token authentication)

aiProvider.description: Selected AI provider for document analysis

openaiKey.description: API key for OpenAI (required when aiProvider is 'openai')

openaiModel.description: OpenAI model to use for analysis

ollamaUrl.description: URL for Ollama API (required when aiProvider is 'ollama')

ollamaModel.description: Ollama model to use for analysis

customApiKey.description: API key for custom LLM provider

customBaseUrl.description: Base URL for custom LLM provider

customModel.description: Model name for custom LLM provider

scanInterval.description: Interval in minutes for scanning new documents

systemPrompt.description: Custom system prompt for document analysis
systemPrompt.example: Extract key information from the following document...

showTags.description: Whether to show tags in the UI

tokenLimit.description: The maximum number of tokens th AI can handle

responseTokens.description: The approx. amount of tokens required for the response

tags.description: Comma-separated list of tags to use for filtering

aiProcessedTag.description: Whether to add a tag for AI-processed documents

aiTagName.description: Tag name to use for AI-processed documents

usePromptTags.description: Whether to use tags in prompts

promptTags.description: Comma-separated list of tags to use in prompts

useExistingData.description: Whether to use existing data from a previous setup

customFields.description: JSON string defining custom fields to extract

message.example: Invalid settings: AI provider required when automatic processing is enabled
Failed to update settings: Database error

get.description:
Returns the currently active application API key for authenticated users.
The key is intentionally fetched on-demand and is not embedded in server-rendered HTML.

configured.description: Indicates whether an API key is currently configured

apiKey.description: Current API key value when configured

get.summary: Get MFA status for current user
get.description: Returns whether TOTP MFA is enabled for the authenticated settings user.

post.summary: Start MFA setup and return provisioning data
post.description: Validates current password and creates a temporary TOTP setup challenge including local QR image data.

post.summary: Enable MFA after validating TOTP code
post.description: Validates current password and setup token, verifies TOTP code, then enables MFA.

post.summary: Verify a TOTP code for an already enabled MFA setup
post.description: Validates an entered TOTP code against the stored user MFA secret.
Validates current password and a valid TOTP code, then disables MFA for the user.

get.description: Detects and returns the public base URL used for document links.

post.description:
Analyzes document content using the configured AI provider and returns structured metadata.
This endpoint processes the document text to extract relevant information such as tags,
correspondent, and document type based on content analysis.

The analysis is performed using the AI provider configured in the application settings.

content.description: The document text content to analyze
content.example: Invoice from Acme Corp. Total amount: $125.00, Due date: 2023-08-15

existingTags.description: List of existing tags in the system to help with tag matching

id.description: Optional document ID for tracking metrics

500.description: Server error or AI provider not configured

post.summary: Process document using a custom prompt in playground mode
post.description:
Analyzes document content using a custom user-provided prompt.
This endpoint is primarily used for testing and experimenting with different prompts
without affecting the actual document processing workflow.

The analysis is performed using the AI provider configured in the application settings,
but with a custom prompt that overrides the default system prompt.

content.description: The document text content to analyze
content.example: Invoice from Acme Corp. Total amount: $125.00, Due date: 2023-08-15

prompt.description: Custom prompt to use for analysis
prompt.example: Extract the company name, invoice amount, and due date from this document.

documentId.description: Optional document ID for tracking metrics

200.description: Document analysis results using the custom prompt

result.description: The raw AI response using the custom prompt
result.example:
Company: Acme Corp
Amount: $125.00
Due Date: 2023-08-15

500.description: Server error or AI provider not configured

post.description:
Updates document metadata such as tags, correspondent and title in the Paperless-ngx system.
This endpoint handles the translation between tag names and IDs, and manages the creation of
new tags or correspondents if they don't exist in the system.

The endpoint also removes any unused tags from the document to keep the metadata clean.

documentId.description: ID of the document to update in Paperless-ngx

tags.description: List of tags to apply (can be tag IDs or names)

correspondent.description: Correspondent name to assign to the document

400.description: Invalid request parameters or tag processing errors

example: Failed to create tag: Invalid tag name

get.description:
Returns the current status of document processing operations.
This endpoint provides information about documents in the processing queue
and the current processing state (active/idle).

The status information can be used by UIs to display progress indicators
and provide real-time feedback about background processing operations.

isProcessing.description: Whether documents are currently being processed

isScanning.description: Whether a scan loop is currently running

stopRequested.description: Whether a graceful stop has been requested

currentlyProcessing.description: Details about the document currently being processed (if any)

post.description: Starts OCR processing for one document and streams progress via SSE.

post.summary: Process all pending OCR queue items
post.description: Starts batch OCR processing and streams progress via SSE.

post.summary: Analyze existing OCR text with AI
post.description: Uses existing OCR text and streams analysis progress via SSE.

get.summary: Get OCR text for one queue item
Check whether the What's New modal should be shown
get.description: Returns show=true when the authenticated user has not yet seen the current release changelog.

post.summary: Mark the current changelog as seen for the authenticated user

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: User authentication and authorization endpoints, including login, logout, and token management

tags.name: Documents
tags.description: Document management and processing endpoints for interacting with Paperless-ngx documents

tags.name: History
tags.description: Document processing history and tracking of AI-generated metadata

tags.name: Navigation
tags.description: General navigation endpoints for the web interface

tags.name: System
tags.description: System configuration, health checks, and administrative functions

tags.name: Metadata
tags.description: Endpoints for managing document metadata like tags, correspondents, and document types
